project.name: aigos-research-worker
project.description: Background research worker for AIGOS that runs Anthropic sub-agents asynchronously to perform marketing research, competitor analysis, and content generation.

nodes.label: .dockerignore
nodes.summary: Specifies files and directories to exclude from the Docker build context. Prevents unnecessary files from being sent to the Docker daemon during image builds.
nodes.name: .dockerignore

nodes.label: .env.example
nodes.summary: Template listing required environment variables for the research worker including API keys for Anthropic, Supabase, Railway, Firecrawl, and SpyFu. Does not contain actual values.
nodes.name: .env.example

nodes.label: .railwayignore
nodes.summary: Specifies files to exclude from Railway deployment uploads, keeping deployment artifacts minimal. Complements Dockerfile for Railway-specific deployments.
nodes.name: .railwayignore

nodes.label: BOOTSTRAP.md
nodes.summary: First-run onboarding guide for a fresh agent instance to establish its identity, name, vibe, and user context by filling in IDENTITY.md and USER.md. Intended to be deleted after the initial setup conversation.
nodes.name: BOOTSTRAP.md

nodes.label: Dockerfile
nodes.summary: Two-stage Docker build (builder + runner) using Node 20 Alpine, compiles TypeScript and runs the worker on port 3001. Copies skill and script reference files into the dist output.
nodes.name: Dockerfile

nodes.label: HEARTBEAT.md
nodes.summary: Configuration file for periodic agent health-check tasks; when empty the agent skips heartbeat API calls, and tasks listed here trigger periodic checks. Acts as a lightweight cron-style task list.
nodes.name: HEARTBEAT.md

nodes.label: IDENTITY.md
nodes.summary: Template for the agent to record its name, creature type, vibe, emoji, and avatar after the bootstrap conversation. Serves as persistent identity memory for the agent across sessions.
nodes.name: IDENTITY.md

nodes.label: package.json
nodes.summary: Node.js package manifest for the aigos-research-worker service, declaring dependencies on Anthropic AI SDK, Supabase, Express, Firecrawl, Zod, and Vercel AI SDK with build/dev/test scripts.
nodes.name: package.json

nodes.label: SOUL.md
nodes.summary: Defines the agent's core behavioral principles: genuine helpfulness over performative, having opinions, being resourceful before asking, and maintaining trust and privacy. Covers boundaries, vibe guidelines, and session continuity via file-based memory.
nodes.name: SOUL.md

nodes.label: EVAL-PROTOCOL.md
nodes.summary: Quality evaluation protocol for ad script generation, defining baseline scores, post-change eval steps, target improvement deltas, and the list of changes applied to improve script quality from C to B average. Used to track ad scripting pipeline improvements.
nodes.name: EVAL-PROTOCOL.md

nodes.label: haynes-frameworks.md
nodes.summary: Reference guide for 7 direct-response video script frameworks (Talking Head, Case Study, Objection-First, Q&A, Demo, 2-Person Interview, Skit) along with Haynes meta-strategies for in-market targeting and creative refresh. Used by the ad-scripts runner to structure video ad content.
nodes.name: haynes-frameworks.md

nodes.label: in-market-segments.md
nodes.summary: Audience segmentation framework dividing markets into three tiers (in-market 3-4%, needs convinced 30%, cold mass) with sub-segments and mapping to Schwartz awareness levels. Guides media plan and targeting strategy generation.
nodes.name: in-market-segments.md

nodes.label: kill-list.json
nodes.summary: Tiered list of banned AI-sounding phrases, filler constructions, sycophantic affirmations, hyphenated corporate buzzwords, and structural patterns to eliminate from generated ad scripts. Applied during the humanization pass of script generation.
nodes.name: kill-list.json

nodes.label: platform-limits.json
nodes.summary: Character and format limits for ad creative across Meta, Google, LinkedIn, TikTok, and email platforms. Referenced during script and copy generation to ensure output stays within platform constraints.
nodes.name: platform-limits.json

nodes.label: ad-copy-templates.md
nodes.summary: Comprehensive reference of ad copywriting frameworks (PAS, BAB, AIDA), headline templates for Google RSA/Meta/LinkedIn, social proof patterns, urgency formulas, CTA best practices, and A/B testing structures. Used by script runners as a skills reference.
nodes.name: ad-copy-templates.md

nodes.label: audience-targeting.md
nodes.summary: Detailed reference covering audience targeting capabilities and best practices across Google Ads, Meta, LinkedIn, and TikTok including keyword match types, custom audiences, and lookalikes. Used by research runners to inform media planning and targeting recommendations.
nodes.name: audience-targeting.md

nodes.label: benchmarks.md
nodes.summary: Performance benchmarks (CTR, CPC, ROAS, conversion rates) by industry and platform to calibrate media plan recommendations. Referenced when generating media plans and evaluating campaign performance expectations.
nodes.name: benchmarks.md

nodes.label: bidding-strategies.md
nodes.summary: Reference guide for bidding strategies across Google, Meta, and LinkedIn including manual CPC, target CPA, ROAS bidding, and budget pacing rules. Informs media plan bidding recommendations in the research pipeline.
nodes.name: bidding-strategies.md

nodes.label: budget-allocation.md
nodes.summary: Framework for allocating ad budgets across channels, funnel stages, and campaign types based on business goals and industry norms. Used during media plan generation to recommend budget splits.
nodes.name: budget-allocation.md

nodes.label: compliance.md
nodes.summary: Ad compliance rules and restrictions for regulated industries (finance, healthcare, legal) across major platforms including prohibited claims and required disclosures. Referenced during ad script and copy generation to ensure compliant output.
nodes.name: compliance.md

nodes.label: conversion-tracking.md
nodes.summary: Reference covering conversion tracking setup for Google Ads, Meta Pixel, LinkedIn Insight Tag, and TikTok Pixel with attribution models and event configuration. Used to inform measurement recommendations in media plans.
nodes.name: conversion-tracking.md

nodes.label: platform-specs.md
nodes.summary: Technical specifications for ad formats across Meta, Google, LinkedIn, TikTok, and YouTube including image dimensions, video length limits, and file size constraints. Used by script and copy runners to generate spec-compliant creative.
nodes.name: platform-specs.md

nodes.label: agency.md
nodes.summary: Ad script and media plan template specialized for marketing and advertising agencies, covering agency-specific value propositions, client acquisition messaging, and positioning. One of multiple vertical-specific templates used by the ad-scripts runner.
nodes.name: agency.md

nodes.label: b2b-enterprise.md
nodes.summary: B2B enterprise-focused ad script template covering longer sales cycles, multi-stakeholder buying, ROI-driven messaging, and enterprise pain points. Applied when the ICP analysis identifies an enterprise B2B business model.
nodes.name: b2b-enterprise.md

nodes.label: ecommerce.md
nodes.summary: E-commerce specific ad script template addressing product discovery, social proof, urgency, and shopping intent signals. Used for DTC and product-based businesses identified during research.
nodes.name: ecommerce.md

nodes.label: finance.md
nodes.summary: Finance industry ad script template with compliance-aware messaging patterns for fintech, investment, insurance, and lending products. Incorporates compliance.md constraints for regulated financial advertising.
nodes.name: finance.md

nodes.label: generic.md
nodes.summary: General-purpose ad script template used when no vertical-specific template matches the business type. Provides universal copywriting patterns adaptable to any industry.
nodes.name: generic.md

nodes.label: healthcare.md
nodes.summary: Healthcare-specific ad script template with HIPAA-aware language, patient journey messaging, and compliance-conscious copy patterns for medical and health services. Incorporates compliance.md constraints.
nodes.name: healthcare.md

nodes.label: info-products.md
nodes.summary: Info product and course creator ad script template emphasizing transformation, credibility, objection handling, and direct response conversion tactics. Tailored to coaches, consultants, and online educators.
nodes.name: info-products.md

nodes.label: local-service.md
nodes.summary: Local service business ad script template covering geo-targeting messaging, trust signals, and call-to-action patterns for service area businesses. Used for plumbers, contractors, dentists, and similar local businesses.
nodes.name: local-service.md

nodes.label: mobile-app.md
nodes.summary: Mobile app ad script template focused on app store conversion, feature demonstration, and user acquisition messaging for iOS and Android apps. Addresses app install campaign creative requirements.
nodes.name: mobile-app.md

nodes.label: real-estate.md
nodes.summary: Real estate ad script template for agents, brokerages, and proptech companies covering buyer/seller journey messaging, local market authority, and lead generation CTAs.
nodes.name: real-estate.md

nodes.label: saas.md
nodes.summary: SaaS ad script template emphasizing trial activation, feature-led growth, ROI proof, and competitive differentiation for software products. Used when ICP analysis identifies a SaaS business model.
nodes.name: saas.md

nodes.label: test-transcript.txt
nodes.summary: Test transcript fixture used for development and evaluation of the research worker pipeline. Provides sample input data for testing runners without requiring live API calls.
nodes.name: test-transcript.txt

nodes.label: tsconfig.json
nodes.summary: TypeScript compiler configuration targeting ES2022/CommonJS with strict mode enabled, compiling from src/ to dist/. Enables JSON module resolution required for importing reference JSON files.
nodes.name: tsconfig.json

nodes.label: TOOLS.md
nodes.summary: Local notes template for the agent to record environment-specific tool configurations such as camera names, SSH hosts, TTS preferences, and device nicknames that are unique to the deployment. Kept separate from shared skills.
nodes.name: TOOLS.md

nodes.label: USER.md
nodes.summary: Template for the agent to record information about the human user including name, pronouns, timezone, and ongoing context notes. Built up over time to enable more personalized assistance.
nodes.name: USER.md

nodes.summary: Environment variable configuration file for the research worker, holding API tokens and service keys required at runtime.
nodes.name: .env

nodes.summary: Standalone CLI test harness for the Apify and SearchAPI ad-scraping pipeline, supporting side-by-side comparison of both providers across Meta, Google, and LinkedIn platforms.
nodes.name: test-ads.ts
printInsight
nodes.summary: Formats and prints a WorkerAdInsight result to stdout, including summary stats, platform sources, library links, and optional verbose ad creatives.

nodes.name: printComparison
nodes.summary: Renders a side-by-side table comparing Apify and SearchAPI ad insights, highlighting unique headlines found in each source.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: CLI entry point that parses flags, selects the ad-fetch mode (compare, apify-only, searchapi-only, or fallback), and dispatches to the appropriate tool functions.
Vitest tests for the ad-scripts pass-1 prompt builder, verifying angle deduplication injection and prompt structure with minimal valid options.
nodes.name: ad-scripts-angles.test.ts

nodes.summary: Vitest tests for the ad library tools, exercising ad candidate matching, deduplication, and insight building across Meta, Google, and LinkedIn sources.
nodes.name: adlibrary.test.ts

nodes.summary: Vitest tests for the worker authorization middleware, covering missing-key, development-loopback, matched-key, and invalid-key scenarios.
nodes.name: auth.test.ts

nodes.summary: Vitest tests for the baseline metrics module, verifying prompt block rendering and context extraction with various missing and provided metric combinations.
nodes.name: baseline-metrics.test.ts

nodes.summary: Vitest tests for the brand-voice prompt builders (pass-1 and pass-2), verifying that style-reference and tone instructions are correctly injected.
nodes.name: brand-voice-prompt.test.ts

nodes.summary: Vitest tests for the chart tool, verifying chart spec normalization and rendering across different chart module shapes.
nodes.name: chart.test.ts

nodes.summary: Vitest tests for the competitors runner, covering timeout detection, retry/fallback/rescue logic, and the full research flow with injected dependencies.
nodes.name: competitors.test.ts

nodes.summary: Vitest tests for the runner contracts module (finalizeRunnerResult), covering all research sections including backward-compatibility scenarios.
nodes.name: contracts.test.ts

nodes.summary: Minimal Vitest tests for the dead-letter queue mechanism that persists failed job records.
nodes.name: dead-letter.test.ts

nodes.summary: Vitest tests for the JSON extraction utility used by the runner to parse structured AI output from raw text responses.
nodes.name: extract-json.test.ts

nodes.summary: Vitest evaluation tests for the identity resolver, checking scoring and field completeness of resolved identity cards against expected outputs.
nodes.name: identity-eval.test.ts

nodes.summary: Vitest tests for the industry runner, verifying retry/repair/rescue logic and the full research flow with mocked dependencies.
nodes.name: industry.test.ts

nodes.summary: Comprehensive Vitest tests for the keyword intelligence runner, covering repair/rescue retry conditions and the full research pipeline with extensive fixture data.
nodes.name: keywords.test.ts

nodes.summary: Vitest tests for the ad library name-matching utility, verifying fuzzy matching of company names against advertiser identifiers.
nodes.name: name-matcher.test.ts

nodes.summary: Vitest tests for the offer analysis runner, covering retry decisions and full runner invocation with mocked telemetry and AI responses.
nodes.name: offer.test.ts

nodes.summary: Vitest tests for the proof-rotation logic in the ad-scripts runner, verifying that social proof elements rotate correctly across generated ad variants.
nodes.name: proof-rotation.test.ts

nodes.summary: Vitest tests for the identity resolution module, verifying parsing and validation of Anthropic API responses into identity card structures.
nodes.name: resolve-identity.test.ts

nodes.summary: Vitest tests for the core runner module, covering progress reporting, timeout handling, and multi-phase execution coordination.
nodes.name: runner.test.ts

nodes.summary: Vitest tests for the script sanitization logic in the ad-scripts runner, verifying that low-quality or malformed ad scripts are filtered out.
nodes.name: sanitize-scripts.test.ts

nodes.summary: Vitest tests for the Supabase client module, verifying upsert operations and realtime update writes for research section results.
nodes.name: supabase.test.ts

nodes.summary: Vitest tests for the synthesis runner, verifying cross-analysis insight generation with mocked AI objects and expected structured output.
nodes.name: synthesize.test.ts

nodes.summary: Worker authorization module that validates incoming requests via API key comparison or development-loopback detection, exporting types and the main authorize function.
nodes.name: auth.ts
authorizeWorkerRequest
nodes.summary: Main authorization entry point that checks for missing key, development loopback, or Bearer token match, returning a structured decision object.
Defines the BaselineMetrics shape and provides utilities to render a prompt injection block and extract metrics from worker context, ensuring AI runners never fabricate CAC/LTV/growth-rate values.
nodes.name: baseline-metrics.ts
renderBaselineMetricsBlock
nodes.summary: Renders a BASELINE METRICS DATA INTEGRITY prompt block injected into runner system prompts to constrain LTV/CAC/growth-rate hallucination.

nodes.name: readBaselineMetricsFromContext
nodes.summary: Extracts and type-narrows a BaselineMetrics object from an opaque worker context payload, returning undefined when metrics are absent.
Test file for competitor review injection logic in synthesize.ts and parallel-fetch.ts. Contains helper factories for building synthesis inputs and parsed competitor outputs.
nodes.name: src/competitors/__tests__/inject-reviews.test.ts

nodes.summary: Test file for review gap intelligence analysis. Tests competitor review gap detection with helper factory for review results.
nodes.name: src/competitors/__tests__/review-gap-intelligence.test.ts

nodes.summary: Main entry point for the competitors research module. Orchestrates validate-then-fetch, synthesis, and full pipeline phases. Exports the main runResearchCompetitors function.
nodes.name: src/competitors/index.ts

nodes.summary: Validates competitors then fetches data in parallel. Core first phase of the competitors pipeline.
nodes.name: runValidateThenFetch

nodes.summary: Runs the synthesis phase on fetched competitor input.
nodes.name: runSynthesis

nodes.summary: Orchestrates the full competitors pipeline: validate, fetch, synthesize.
nodes.name: runPipeline

nodes.summary: Top-level exported function to run the full competitor research flow with progress reporting.
nodes.name: runResearchCompetitors

nodes.summary: Parallel data fetcher for competitor intelligence. Fetches pricing pages, SpyFu keyword data, ad library ads, and customer reviews concurrently for each competitor.
nodes.name: src/competitors/parallel-fetch.ts

nodes.summary: Fetches and extracts pricing page content for a competitor using Firecrawl.
nodes.name: fetchPricing

nodes.summary: Fetches SpyFu keyword intelligence for a competitor.
nodes.name: fetchSpyfu

nodes.summary: Fetches ad library data for a competitor.
nodes.name: fetchAdLibrary

nodes.summary: Orchestrates parallel fetching of all data sources for all competitors. Main exported fetch orchestrator.
nodes.name: fetchAllCompetitorData

nodes.summary: Builds timeout placeholder results when fetch phase times out.
nodes.name: buildTimeoutResults

nodes.summary: Parses raw competitor context strings into structured data. Extracts competitor entries, identity cards, and parsed competitor context from unstructured context strings.
nodes.name: src/competitors/parse-context.ts

nodes.summary: Parses a raw competitor string into structured CompetitorEntry objects.
nodes.name: parseCompetitorString

nodes.summary: Main exported parser that extracts all structured competitor data from a raw context string.
nodes.name: parseCompetitorContext

nodes.summary: Analyzes review patterns across multiple competitors to identify common weaknesses. Uses Claude to extract cross-competitor vulnerability patterns.
nodes.name: src/competitors/review-cross-analysis.ts

nodes.summary: Main exported function that analyzes review patterns across all competitors to find common weaknesses.
nodes.name: analyzeReviewCrossPatterns

nodes.summary: Identifies exploit angles from competitor negative reviews. Analyzes gaps in competitor offerings to surface positioning opportunities for the client.
nodes.name: src/competitors/review-gap-intelligence.ts

nodes.summary: Main exported function. Analyzes negative reviews across competitors to identify positioning gaps the subject company can exploit.
nodes.name: analyzeReviewGaps

nodes.summary: Discovers and validates competitors using Perplexity/Sonar AI search. Verifies domain resolution, validates competitor relevance, and fetches structured competitor research.
nodes.name: src/competitors/sonar-research.ts

nodes.summary: Discovers competitor candidates from a company identity card using Sonar AI search.
nodes.name: discoverCompetitorsFromIdentity

nodes.summary: Main exported function. Runs full Sonar-based competitor research including discovery and deep per-competitor research.
nodes.name: fetchSonarCompetitorResearch

nodes.summary: Validates a batch of competitor candidates for relevance.
nodes.name: validateCompetitors

nodes.summary: Synthesizes raw competitor intelligence into structured output. Builds evidence packages, injects reviews and ad library links, and runs Claude to produce final competitor intel.
nodes.name: src/competitors/synthesize.ts

nodes.summary: Assembles all fetched competitor data into a structured evidence package for the synthesis prompt.
nodes.name: buildEvidencePackage

nodes.summary: Exported function. Runs Claude to synthesize competitor intelligence from the evidence package.
nodes.name: synthesizeCompetitorIntel

nodes.summary: Injects review gap intelligence and cross-analysis data into parsed synthesis output.
nodes.name: injectReviews

nodes.summary: Exported function. Post-processes synthesis output by injecting reviews, cross-analysis, and client ads.
nodes.name: postProcessSynthesis

nodes.summary: Central contracts file defining Zod schemas, normalizers, and utility functions for all research section payloads. Includes media plan schemas, competitor intel normalization, telemetry building, and runner result finalization.
nodes.name: src/contracts.ts

nodes.summary: Normalizes raw competitor intel payload data into the canonical structure.
nodes.name: normalizeCompetitorIntelPayload

nodes.summary: Exported function. Finalizes a runner result by normalizing, validating, writing to Supabase, and emitting telemetry.
nodes.name: finalizeRunnerResult

nodes.summary: Splits a large research payload into separate section records for Supabase storage.
nodes.name: splitPayload

nodes.summary: Builds telemetry metadata from raw telemetry and parsed section data.
nodes.name: buildTelemetry

nodes.summary: Dead letter queue writer for failed research jobs. Persists failed job data to disk for later inspection or replay.
nodes.name: src/dead-letter.ts

nodes.summary: Exported function. Writes a failed job payload to the dead letter queue on disk.
nodes.name: writeDeadLetter

nodes.summary: Diagnostic evaluation script for testing ad library API search accuracy across LinkedIn, Meta, and Google ad platforms. Not part of production pipeline.
nodes.name: src/eval/diagnostic-spike.ts

nodes.summary: End-to-end evaluation script for testing directive-based ad extraction via Apify.
nodes.name: src/eval/e2e-directive-test.ts

nodes.summary: Evaluation script for testing ad relevance matching across Meta, Google, and LinkedIn ad platforms using the ad library tools.
nodes.name: src/eval/test-ad-relevance.ts

nodes.summary: Resolves product/company identity from raw context strings. Uses Claude to extract structured identity cards with fallback construction if AI fails.
nodes.name: src/identity/resolve-identity.ts

nodes.summary: Main exported function. Uses Claude to resolve structured identity from context with fallback.
nodes.name: resolveProductIdentity

nodes.summary: Express HTTP server entry point for the research worker. Handles job dispatch routing, authentication middleware, job queue management, and health checks. Exports the Express app.
nodes.name: src/index.ts

nodes.summary: Express middleware that validates the RAILWAY_API_KEY on incoming requests.
nodes.name: requireApiKey

nodes.summary: Queues a job status write to Supabase after job completion.
nodes.name: queueJobStatusWrite

nodes.summary: Strategic planning module using Claude Opus to generate structured plans (e.g., ad strategy) from research context.
nodes.name: src/planning/opus-planner.ts

nodes.summary: Exported function. Calls Claude Opus to generate a strategic plan from context and plan type.
nodes.name: getStrategicPlan

nodes.summary: Prompt builder for the first pass of ad script generation. Constructs the detailed system and user prompt for draft ad script creation.
nodes.name: src/prompts/ad-scripts-pass1.ts

nodes.summary: Exported function. Builds the full prompt for pass 1 ad script generation.
nodes.name: buildPass1Prompt

nodes.summary: Prompt builder for the second pass of ad script generation (humanization/refinement with 43-point audit).
nodes.name: src/prompts/ad-scripts-pass2.ts

nodes.summary: Exported function. Builds the full prompt for pass 2 ad script humanization.
nodes.name: buildPass2Prompt

nodes.summary: Core runner utilities and abstractions. Provides Anthropic client creation, progress reporting, tool block parsing, JSON extraction, streaming tool runner orchestration, and backoff retry logic.
nodes.name: src/runner.ts

nodes.summary: Creates and returns a configured Anthropic SDK client.
nodes.name: createClient

nodes.summary: Emits a progress update through the onProgress callback with phase and message.
nodes.name: emitRunnerProgress

nodes.summary: Exported function. Runs a streaming tool-use runner with the Anthropic SDK and collects results.
nodes.name: runStreamedToolRunner

nodes.summary: Extracts draft fact messages from a streaming snapshot for mid-stream progress updates.
nodes.name: extractDraftFactMessages

nodes.summary: Sanitizes values for safe JSON serialization.
nodes.name: sanitizeForJson

nodes.summary: Extracts JSON from a text string that may contain markdown fences or extra content.
nodes.name: extractJson

nodes.summary: Test file for guardrail prompt validation across synthesize, media-plan, and offer runners.
nodes.name: src/runners/__tests__/guardrail-prompts.test.ts

nodes.summary: Runner for two-pass ad script generation. Orchestrates draft generation (pass 1) and humanization (pass 2) with deduplication, diversity validation, and proof point tracking.
nodes.name: src/runners/ad-scripts.ts

nodes.summary: Main exported function. Runs the two-pass ad script generation pipeline with level-based completion tracking.
nodes.name: runAdScripts

nodes.summary: Deduplicates generated scripts by content similarity.
nodes.name: dedupScripts

nodes.summary: Validates that a batch of ad scripts has sufficient diversity across formats.
nodes.name: validateBatchDiversity

nodes.summary: High-level competitors runner with primary/fallback/rescue attempt modes, timeout handling, repair logic, and observability. Orchestrates the full competitors research pipeline with retry strategies.
nodes.name: src/runners/competitors.ts

nodes.summary: Exported function. Main entry point for running competitor research with full retry/fallback logic.
nodes.name: runResearchCompetitors

nodes.summary: Exported function. Runs competitor research with injectable dependencies for testing.
nodes.name: runResearchCompetitorsWithDeps

nodes.summary: Handles competitor repair phase with timeout fallback strategies.
nodes.name: runCompetitorRepairWithTimeoutFallback

nodes.summary: Runs a competitor attempt with full observability logging and telemetry.
nodes.name: runCompetitorAttemptWithObservability

nodes.summary: Builds recovery context for repair/rescue competitor attempts from prior attempt results.
nodes.name: buildCompetitorRecoveryContext

nodes.summary: Runner for Ideal Customer Profile (ICP) research section. Uses Claude with web search tools to generate ICP validation analysis.
nodes.name: src/runners/icp.ts

nodes.summary: Exported function. Runs ICP research using Claude with intelligence skill scaffolding.
nodes.name: runResearchICP

nodes.summary: Barrel re-export index for the runners module.
nodes.name: src/runners/index.ts

nodes.label: industry.ts
nodes.summary: Research runner for industry/market analysis. Implements a multi-phase pipeline (primary, repair, rescue) using AI to generate structured industry intelligence with market size normalization and deduplication.
nodes.name: industry.ts

nodes.label: keywords.ts
nodes.summary: Largest runner (1494 lines) for keyword intelligence research. Supports tool-based and message-based attempts, provider status checks (Google Ads, SpyFu), recovery context building, competitor keyword detection, and artifact thinness validation.
nodes.name: keywords.ts

nodes.label: media-plan.ts
nodes.summary: Runner that generates a full media plan by orchestrating multiple skill blocks (channel mix, audience, creative system, measurement, rollout, strategy snapshot). Each block is generated sequentially via opus-planner.
nodes.name: media-plan.ts

nodes.label: meeting-extract.ts
nodes.summary: Runner that extracts structured intelligence from meeting transcripts. Thin wrapper around a single AI extraction call with progress reporting.
nodes.name: meeting-extract.ts

nodes.label: offer.ts
nodes.summary: Runner for offer/value-proposition analysis research. Supports optional Firecrawl web scraping for first-party URL data, with primary/repair/rescue attempt phases.
nodes.name: offer.ts

nodes.label: synthesize.ts
nodes.summary: Cross-analysis synthesis runner that compiles all prior research sections into a unified intelligence artifact. Includes telemetry tracking for token usage.
nodes.name: synthesize.ts

nodes.label: ad-scripts.ts
nodes.summary: Zod schemas for ad script generation: adScriptGenerateSchema, awarenessLevelOutputSchema, and the AdScriptGenerate type.
nodes.name: ad-scripts.ts

nodes.label: claim-extractor.test.ts
nodes.summary: Vitest tests for the claim extractor stage of the ad scripts pipeline.
nodes.name: claim-extractor.test.ts

nodes.label: planner.test.ts
nodes.summary: Vitest tests for the script matrix planner (stage 01) of the ad scripts pipeline.
nodes.name: planner.test.ts

nodes.label: quality-gate.test.ts
nodes.summary: Vitest tests for the quality gate stage that validates ad scripts against kill lists and style rules.
nodes.name: quality-gate.test.ts

nodes.label: pipeline.ts
nodes.summary: Orchestrator for the full ad scripts pipeline. Runs stages: plan → claims → creative write → quality gate. Exports types PipelineInput, AssembledScript, DynamicCreativePackage, and the main runScriptPipeline function.
nodes.name: pipeline.ts

nodes.label: loader.ts
nodes.summary: Loads static reference data files: kill lists (banned phrases/patterns), platform character limits, Haynes frameworks, and in-market segments. Used by quality gate.
nodes.name: loader.ts

nodes.label: planner.ts
nodes.summary: Stage 01 of the scripts pipeline. Builds a script matrix of diverse ScriptPlan objects covering awareness levels, in-market tiers, angles, formats, and platforms. Validates diversity across the matrix.
nodes.name: planner.ts

nodes.label: claim-extractor.ts
nodes.summary: Stage 02 of the scripts pipeline. Extracts categorized claims (stats, proof points, differentiators) from research context for use in script writing.
nodes.name: claim-extractor.ts

nodes.label: hook-generator.ts
nodes.summary: Stage 03a of the scripts pipeline. Generates attention-grabbing hooks for ad scripts using claims and script plan context.
nodes.name: hook-generator.ts

nodes.label: creative-writer.ts
nodes.summary: Stage 03b of the scripts pipeline. Writes full creative ad scripts (hook + body) for a given awareness level using the plan, claims, and skills context.
nodes.name: creative-writer.ts

nodes.label: body-writer.ts
nodes.summary: Stage 04 of the scripts pipeline. Writes the body copy for ad scripts, with format-specific schemas (video, static, search). Complements creative-writer for structured body output.
nodes.name: body-writer.ts

nodes.label: quality-gate.ts
nodes.summary: Stage 05 of the scripts pipeline. Runs 43-point quality audit on ad scripts: kill list checks, char limits, sentence rhythm, rule-of-three, transitions, chatbot pattern detection, and hyphenated corporate jargon.
nodes.name: quality-gate.ts

nodes.label: voice-polisher.ts
nodes.summary: Stage 06 of the scripts pipeline. Applies final voice and tone polish to ad scripts after quality gate, merging polish results back into the original script structure.
nodes.name: voice-polisher.ts

nodes.label: section-map.ts
nodes.summary: Maps tool section names to canonical research section keys. Exports TOOL_SECTION_MAP, normalizeResearchSection, and normalizeResearchRecord utility functions.
nodes.name: section-map.ts

nodes.label: audience-campaign-skill.ts
nodes.summary: Exports AUDIENCE_CAMPAIGN_SKILL — a static skill prompt/config for audience campaign planning used by the media plan runner.
nodes.name: audience-campaign-skill.ts

nodes.label: channel-mix-skill.ts
nodes.summary: Exports CHANNEL_MIX_SKILL — a static skill prompt/config for channel mix recommendations used by the media plan runner.
nodes.name: channel-mix-skill.ts

nodes.label: creative-system-skill.ts
nodes.summary: Exports CREATIVE_SYSTEM_SKILL — a static skill prompt/config for creative systems (ad formats, creative frameworks) used by the media plan runner.
nodes.name: creative-system-skill.ts

nodes.label: intelligence-skill.ts
nodes.summary: Exports five intelligence skill prompts used by industry and synthesize runners: INDUSTRY_INTELLIGENCE_SKILL, ICP_INTELLIGENCE_SKILL, COMPETITORS_INTELLIGENCE_SKILL, COMPETITORS_INTELLIGENCE_SKILL_COMPACT, SYNTHESIS_INTELLIGENCE_SKILL.
nodes.name: intelligence-skill.ts

nodes.summary: Orchestrates the full ad scripts pipeline across all stages for all awareness levels, with per-level completion callbacks.

nodes.summary: Validates that the generated script matrix has sufficient diversity across dimensions.

nodes.summary: Extracts and categorizes claims (stats, proof, differentiators) from research context (188 lines).

nodes.summary: Formats selected extracted claims into a string suitable for inclusion in script prompts.

nodes.summary: Generates ad script hooks using AI, given a HookGeneratorInput with plan and claims.

nodes.summary: Builds the full creative prompt (237 lines) for the AI writer, incorporating plan, claims, and skill context.

nodes.label: src/skills/loader.ts
nodes.summary: Skill loader utility that loads skill block references, ref files, and industry templates from the filesystem. Provides helper functions used by the skills system to dynamically load YAML/prompt blocks at runtime.

nodes.label: src/skills/measurement-skill.ts
nodes.summary: Exports the MEASUREMENT_SKILL constant — a static skill definition describing measurement/KPI strategy prompts used by the research pipeline.
nodes.name: src/skills/measurement-skill.ts

nodes.label: src/skills/rollout-skill.ts
nodes.summary: Exports the ROLLOUT_SKILL constant — a static skill definition for go-to-market rollout strategy prompts used in the research pipeline.
nodes.name: src/skills/rollout-skill.ts

nodes.label: src/skills/strategy-snapshot-skill.ts
nodes.summary: Exports the STRATEGY_SNAPSHOT_SKILL constant — a static skill definition for strategy snapshot prompts used in research synthesis.
nodes.name: src/skills/strategy-snapshot-skill.ts

nodes.label: src/supabase.ts
nodes.summary: Core Supabase client and data-write layer for the research worker. Provides functions to write research results, job status updates, and script pack updates to Supabase. Includes retry logic and merge helpers for concurrent job status writes.
nodes.name: src/supabase.ts

nodes.label: src/telemetry.ts
nodes.summary: Telemetry utilities for runner cost and usage tracking. Defines types for model pricing, runner usage/chart telemetry, and exports buildRunnerTelemetry to compute cost estimates from Anthropic API usage data.
nodes.name: src/telemetry.ts

nodes.label: src/tools/adlibrary-types.ts
nodes.summary: Type definitions for the ad library tool: WorkerAdCreative, WorkerLibraryLinks, WorkerAdInsight, and the WorkerAdPlatform enum. Shared types consumed by adlibrary.ts and apify-ads.ts.
nodes.name: src/tools/adlibrary-types.ts

nodes.label: src/tools/adlibrary.ts
nodes.summary: Large ad library aggregator tool (~1344 lines). Searches Google, LinkedIn, Meta, and Foreplay ad libraries via SearchAPI. Includes advertiser matching, candidate resolution, creative normalization, and the adLibraryTool definition. Core competitor ad intelligence engine.
nodes.name: src/tools/adlibrary.ts

nodes.label: src/tools/apify-ads.ts
nodes.summary: Apify-based ad scraper as an alternative/fallback to SearchAPI. Fetches Meta, Google, and LinkedIn ads via Apify actors, normalizes them to WorkerAdCreative format, and provides fetchCompetitorAds as the main entry point.
nodes.name: src/tools/apify-ads.ts

nodes.label: src/tools/chart.ts
nodes.summary: Chart generation tool that normalizes data into chart specs and calls an external chart rendering service (AntV MCP). Supports bar, line, radar, and other chart types via normalizeChartSpec. Exports chartTool for use in runners.
nodes.name: src/tools/chart.ts

nodes.label: src/tools/firecrawl-extract.ts
nodes.summary: Tool definition for Firecrawl structured extraction (LLM-based extraction from URLs). Exports firecrawlExtractTool.
nodes.name: src/tools/firecrawl-extract.ts

nodes.label: src/tools/firecrawl.ts
nodes.summary: Firecrawl web scraping tool. Exports firecrawlTool and a compactFirecrawlMarkdown helper that trims scraped markdown content.
nodes.name: src/tools/firecrawl.ts

nodes.label: src/tools/ga4.ts
nodes.summary: Google Analytics 4 tool. Handles OAuth2 service account authentication and exports ga4Tool for querying GA4 reporting API.
nodes.name: src/tools/ga4.ts

nodes.label: src/tools/google-ads.ts
nodes.summary: Google Ads API tool. Provides OAuth2 token acquisition and GAQL query execution. Exports googleAdsTool for querying Google Ads campaign data.
nodes.name: src/tools/google-ads.ts

nodes.label: src/tools/index.ts
nodes.summary: Barrel index file for the tools directory. Re-exports all tool definitions for centralized import.
nodes.name: src/tools/index.ts

nodes.label: src/tools/meta-ads.ts
nodes.summary: Meta (Facebook) Ads API tool. Queries Meta Graph API for ad campaign performance data. Exports metaAdsTool.
nodes.name: src/tools/meta-ads.ts

nodes.label: src/tools/pagespeed.ts
nodes.summary: Google PageSpeed Insights tool. Exports pagespeedTool to query Core Web Vitals and performance scores for a URL.
nodes.name: src/tools/pagespeed.ts

nodes.label: src/tools/reviews.ts
nodes.summary: Review aggregation tool (~700 lines). Scrapes Trustpilot, G2, and Capterra for ratings and reviews, plus discovers testimonials from company websites. Provides fetchReviews as the main orchestrator and exports reviewsTool.
nodes.name: src/tools/reviews.ts

nodes.label: src/tools/spyfu.ts
nodes.summary: SpyFu SEO competitive intelligence tool. Exports spyfuTool for querying keyword rankings and competitor SEO data.
nodes.name: src/tools/spyfu.ts

nodes.label: src/types/antv-mcp-server-chart-sdk.d.ts

nodes.label: src/utils/name-matcher.ts

nodes.label: src/utils/strip-numeric-constraints.ts
nodes.summary: Zod schema utility that strips numeric constraints (min/max) from Zod schemas before passing them to Anthropic's generateObject API, which rejects such constraints.

nodes.label: src/validators/media-plan.ts
nodes.summary: Zod validation schemas for the media plan output (~511 lines). Defines the full structure of MediaPlan, channel allocations, campaign phases, and budget breakdowns validated from AI-generated output.
nodes.name: src/validators/media-plan.ts

nodes.summary: Normalizes raw SearchAPI ad records to WorkerAdCreative format (~133 lines).

nodes.summary: Main entry point for Apify-based competitor ad fetching (~153 lines). Orchestrates Meta, Google, LinkedIn ad fetches, normalizes, filters, and deduplicates.

nodes.label: discoverAndExtractTestimonials
nodes.summary: Discovers testimonial pages on a company domain and extracts structured testimonials.
nodes.name: discoverAndExtractTestimonials

layers.name: API Layer
layers.description: Express HTTP server entry point, authentication middleware, job queue management, and health check routing. This is the outermost layer that receives requests from the AIGOS frontend and dispatches jobs to research runners.

layers.name: Research Runners
layers.description: High-level runner orchestrators for each research section (industry, ICP, competitors, offer, keywords, synthesize, media-plan, meeting-extract, ad-scripts). Each runner implements a primary/repair/rescue multi-phase retry strategy and writes results via the contracts layer.

layers.name: Competitor Intelligence
layers.description: Dedicated sub-pipeline for competitor research: Perplexity/Sonar discovery, parallel data fetching (pricing, ads, reviews), synthesis via Claude, context parsing, cross-analysis of review gaps, and review pattern detection.

layers.name: Ad Scripts Pipeline
layers.description: Multi-stage ad script generation pipeline: planning a script matrix, claim extraction, hook generation, creative writing, body copy, 43-point quality gate, and voice polish. Also includes prompt builders for the legacy two-pass runner and ad-scripts Zod schemas.

layers.name: Skills & Planning
layers.description: Skill prompt constants (channel mix, audience, creative system, measurement, rollout, strategy snapshot, intelligence, keyword campaign) used by runners as scaffolded expertise blocks. Also includes the Opus-based strategic planner and the skill loader utility.

layers.name: AI Tools & Integrations
layers.description: External API tool wrappers used by runners during research: ad library aggregator (SearchAPI), Apify ad scraper, Firecrawl web scraper, SpyFu SEO intelligence, Google Ads, Meta Ads, GA4, PageSpeed, customer reviews (Trustpilot/G2/Capterra), and chart generation.

layers.name: Data & Persistence
layers.description: Supabase client and write layer, Zod contracts and normalizers for all research section payloads, media plan validator schemas, section mapping utilities, baseline metrics prompt injection, and telemetry cost tracking.

layers.name: Testing & Evaluation
layers.description: Vitest unit and integration tests for all runners, tools, and utilities. Also includes evaluation scripts for ad library accuracy, directive extraction, and ad script quality assessment, plus CLI test harnesses and test fixtures.

layers.name: Configuration & Infrastructure
layers.description: Docker build definition, Railway and Docker ignore files, environment variable template, Node package manifest, TypeScript compiler config, Vitest config, ambient type declarations, and agent identity/persona documents (BOOTSTRAP, IDENTITY, SOUL, USER, TOOLS, HEARTBEAT).

layers.name: Reference Knowledge & Templates
layers.description: Static reference documents and configuration data loaded at runtime: ad copy frameworks, audience targeting guides, performance benchmarks, bidding strategies, budget allocation, compliance rules, conversion tracking, platform specs, kill lists, platform character limits, Haynes video frameworks, in-market segment models, vertical-specific ad script templates (SaaS, ecommerce, finance, healthcare, etc.), and the eval quality protocol.

tour.title: Express Server Entry Point
tour.description: The worker boots as a standalone Express server on port 3001. This file defines the HTTP endpoints, API key middleware, and job queue that accept research dispatch requests from the main Next.js app.

tour.title: Authentication and Request Authorization
tour.description: Incoming requests are gated by a shared Railway API key checked in auth.ts. Understanding this layer shows how the worker stays isolated from unauthorized callers.

tour.title: Core Runner Infrastructure
tour.description: runner.ts is the shared execution engine all research runners call into. It wraps Anthropic streaming tool-use, extracts JSON from model responses, and emits real-time progress events back to Supabase.

tour.title: Research Runner Pipeline
tour.description: Each research section (industry, ICP, offer, keywords, synthesize, media plan) has its own runner in src/runners/. Explore how they follow a primary → repair → rescue pattern and dispatch results to Supabase.

tour.title: External Data Tools
tour.description: Runners pull live data via tools in src/tools/: web scraping via Firecrawl, keyword intelligence via SpyFu, ad creative via ad library APIs, and review aggregation from Trustpilot and G2.

tour.title: Competitor Research Module
tour.description: The competitors module is the most complex sub-system: it discovers competitors via Sonar/Perplexity, fetches pricing and ad data in parallel, analyzes review gaps, and synthesizes everything into a competitive intelligence package.

tour.title: Identity Resolution
tour.description: Before research begins, the worker resolves the company's product identity from a URL by extracting structured fields (category, positioning, ICP) that all downstream runners use as their foundational context.

tour.title: Skills and Reference Knowledge
tour.description: The skills system loads vertical-specific templates (SaaS, ecommerce, B2B, healthcare, etc.) and reference documents (benchmarks, bidding strategies, audience targeting) that ground AI outputs in real-world marketing knowledge.

tour.title: Ad Script Generation Pipeline
tour.description: Ad scripts go through a 6-stage pipeline: plan a script matrix, extract verifiable claims, generate hooks, write creative and body copy, run a quality gate against a kill list, then polish for human voice.

tour.title: Prompts, Schemas, and Anti-Slop
tour.description: Ad script prompts are built from structured templates in src/prompts/ and validated against Zod schemas. The kill-list enforces a humanization pass that strips AI-sounding clichés before results are saved.

tour.title: Data Persistence via Supabase
tour.description: All runner results flow through supabase.ts, which writes research sections and job status updates in real-time so the frontend can receive them via Supabase Realtime subscriptions. The dead-letter queue captures failures.

tour.title: Testing and Evaluation
tour.description: The test suite covers every runner, contract shape, and utility. The eval/ directory contains scripts for measuring ad script quality against a defined protocol, enabling data-driven prompt iteration.
